---
title: "Integrated-circuit building blocks and layout"
chapter-id: A07
status: outline
---

::: {.callout-note title="Chapter outline"}
The scope and dependency boundary are stable. Full exposition, worked examples,
exercises, and tested practical evidence still need authoring and review.
:::

## Central question

What changes when transistors and interconnect are designed together on silicon
rather than assembled as discrete parts on a board?

## Learning outcomes

After completing this chapter, the reader should be able to:

- relate CMOS device behavior to inverter, switch, mirror, differential-pair,
  and gain-stage operation;
- explain the roles of process, geometry, matching, bias, and parasitics;
- read a simplified integrated-circuit schematic and layout as connected physical
  representations;
- use simulation evidence to defend a bounded block-level design decision.

## Concept path

- CMOS process, wells, devices, interconnect, and design rules;
- inverter transfer, noise margin, delay, dynamic power, and leakage;
- current mirrors, differential pairs, active loads, and bias networks;
- gain stages, output stages, common mode, headroom, and stability;
- matching, corners, Monte Carlo analysis, and layout-dependent effects;
- hierarchy, PDKs, schematic/layout verification, packaging, and test.

## Evidence and practice

The completed chapter will include transistor-level simulation across process,
voltage, and temperature corners; a layout-aware parasitic comparison; and a
small block whose performance claim is limited by an explicit error budget.

## Connections

Chapter ID: `A07`. Prerequisites and downstream course mappings are maintained in
[`chapters.toml`](../chapters.toml). The [reading roadmap](../roadmap.qmd)
provides reader-facing routes and the practice spine.
